Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Courts Fee Award And In The Alternative For Order To Show Cause Why Defendants Should Not Be Held In Contempt Of Court
Off Calendar Jan-07-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ROCKY C. TSAI, Esq. of Ropes & Gray LLP (Tel: 415-315-6300) for Plaintiffs;
Attys. WILLIAM S. KRONENBERG, Esq. and V. SATHIENMARS, Esq. of Kronenberg Law PC (Tel: 510-254-6767) for Defendants Valstock Management Company, a corporation; Valstock Ventures, LLC, a limited liability company; 371 Broadway Street, LLC, a limited liability company; Golden Prosperities Management Company, LLC, a limited liability company; Samantha Lee Seto Management, LLC, a limited liability company; Samantha Seto, an individual; and Kelvin Yee, an individual.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS' Motion To Enforce Courts Fee Award And In The Alternative For Order To Show Cause Why Defendants Should Not Be Held In Contempt Of Court.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiffs' motion to enforce court's fee award and, in the alternative, for order to show cause why defendants should not be held in contempt of court is denied. Defendants' request for an order to show cause is pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 128.7 is denied.

On November 18, 2020, this Court granted plaintiffs' motion for attorney's fees in the amount of $1,113,860.75. On December 21, 2020, Defendants filed a notice of appeal from that order. That notice of appeal automatically stayed enforcement of the order without the need for Defendants to file an undertaking. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 917.1(d) ["However, no undertaking shall be required pursuant to this section solely for costs awarded under Chapter 6 (commencing with Section 1021) of Title 14."]; Ziello v. Superior Court (1999) 75 Cal.App.4th 651, 655 [section 917.1(b) "eliminates the requirement of an undertaking when the appeal is solely from an award of costs," including attorney's fees].) Chapala Management Corp. v. Stanton (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 1532 is on point. There, a condominium association prevailed in an action for declaratory and injunctive relief against defendant homeowners; the trial court awarded the association attorney fees and costs, and ordered the homeowners to post a bond or undertaking to stay collection of the fee award. The defendants sought supersedeas relief, which the Court of Appeal granted, holding that the trial court's award of fees was "automatically stayed pending any appeal on grounds the attorney fees awarded are a routine or incidental item of costs, awarded as a matter of right to the prevailing party." (Id. at 1546.) The court reasoned that "if we were to characterize the attorney fee award here as a judgment in substance 'directing the payment of money,' we would write out of section 917.1, subdivision (d) any attorney fees awarded as costs under section 1032. The Legislature has not provided for such an exception, and we will not rewrite the statute to create it."; see also Quiles v. Parent (2017) 10 Cal.App.5th 130, 138-141 ["many attorney fee awards can be part of a 'costs-only' judgment that need not be bonded to stay an appeal," including contractual attorney fees].) Plaintiffs fail to address section 917.1(b) or Chapala, and the cases they rely upon are inapposite. (See Chapala Management Corp., 186 Cal.App.4th at 1546 ["We decline to read Bank of San Pedro . . . as equating all attorney fee awards with expert witness fees . . . . The high court's analysis in Bank of San Pedro was directly solely at the expert fees awarded under section 998, and its reasoning was based on the operation of that statute. Further, Behniwal conceded that its discussion of the issue was not for the purpose of squarely deciding whether or not an undertaking was required . . . ."]; compare Behniwal v. Mix (2007) 147 Cal.App.4th 621, 631 [judgment granting prospective buyers' request for specific performance and ordering sellers' agent to pay attorney fees].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Christine L. Jordan, CSR No. 12262, cjordan@sftc.org (302/EPS)

Defendants Golden Prosperities Managment Company, Llc, Samantha Seto, Kelvin Yee, Samantha Lee Seto Management, Llc, 371 Broadway Street, Llc, Valstock Management Company, Valstock Ventures, Llc'S Motion To Seal Documents Related To Plaintiffs Opposition To Valstocks Msj And Plaintiffs Msa
Off Calendar Sep-23-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Rocky C. Tsai (rocky.tsai@ropesgray.com) and Daniel Richards for Plaintiffs.
V. Sathienmars (vsathienmars@krolaw.com) for Defendants.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants Golden Prosperities Managment Company, LLC, Samantha Seto, Kelvin Yee, Samantha Lee Seto Management, LLC, 371 Broadway Street, LLC, Valstock Management Company, Valstock Ventures, LLC'S Motion To Seal Documents Related To Plaintiffs Opposition To Valstocks MSJ And Plaintiffs MSA:

Defendant Valstock, et al.'s motion to seal is denied without prejudice. Valstock fails to demonstrate that sealing of the Richards and Irvin declarations in support of plaintiffs' opposition to defendants Valstock, et al's motion for summary judgment/adjudication and the Horowitz and Irvin declarations in support of plaintiffs' motion for summary adjudication is proper under Cal. R. Ct. 2.550(d). The requested sealing order is overbroad and sealing is not appropriate merely because the parties entered into a protective order. (See Cal.. R. Ct. 2.551(a) ["The court must not permit a record to be filed under seal based solely on the agreement or stipulation of the parties."]. Valstock has leave to renew its motion in compliance with Cal. R. Ct. 2.550. Valstock's request for sanctions and request to strike documents is denied.

order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Witnesses Alleged In Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-16-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Traci J. Irvin, esq. (415-315-2322) of Ropes & Gray LLP for Plaintiff via Court Call.
No other appearances.

Ms. Irvin reported that she has received notification the moving party has withdrawn their motion as of Friday late afternoon. The Clerk noted the court has not received any courtesy copies of this information. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs GOLDEN PROSPERITIES MANAGMENT COMPANY, LLC, SAMANTHA LEE SETO MANAGMENT, LLC, SAMANTHA SETO, KELVIN YEE, 371 BROADWAY STREET, LLC, VALSTOCK MANAGEMENT COMPANY, VALSTOCK VENTURES, LLC's Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Witnesses Alleged In Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint is Motion to compel DENIED for failure to meet and confer. CCP 2025.450. As counsel for the third-party deponents points out, CCP 2025.450 applies to parties and witnesses designated to appear for parties, and none of these deponents are parties. Regardless, the non-party witnesses served objections under CCP 2025.410, and the statement in the objections that the witnesses would not appear is not a "foreclosure" of efforts to meet and confer, but a reasonable confirmation that the witness would not appear despite the objections. Movants do not provide any evidence of an attempt to meet-and-confer after receipt of the objections. The request for sanctions by counsel for the third-party deponents is DENIED for failure to properly support the request - even though counsel contends that the hourly rates are business confidential information, there is no declaration on the amount of hours worked by each timekeeper or other facts supporting the amount requested as reasonably incurred. CCP 2023.040.

Prevailing party to send proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to sborrowman@gmail.com

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
